steps.title: Use the sample mean
steps.content: Find the sample mean from the five acre counts: $$\frac{51+59+45+52+73}{5}=56$$ So the sample estimates about $56$ trees per acre.

steps.title: Scale to the whole forest
steps.content: Multiply by the forest size: $$56\times253=14{,}168$$ Desmos can do this in one expression by taking the mean of the sample list and multiplying by $253$.

steps.title: Choose the interval
steps.content: $14{,}168$ falls between $13{,}500$ and $14{,}500$. That matches choice C, so C is correct.
